mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration that forces investors to downgrade near-term earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery that lifts corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline with a t-statistic above 1.96, or quarterly forward returns are negative in >50% of co-occurring expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity constraints and rising input costs that compress profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The negative return pattern reverses to positive during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power when filtered by business cycle dating.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index readings force markets to price in tighter monetary policy expectations, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising inflation observations or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A severe drop in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a sharp contraction in economic activity that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances or the threshold fails to separate return distributions at a 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures reveals weakening consumer spending relative to aggregate output, prompting equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in >60% of cases or interaction term coefficients are statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply-side bottlenecks that delay earnings normalization, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of declining production observations within the specified regime or the signal loses predictive power after 2009.
